theme_connections.cluster_narrative: The Face Control Crisis: These pairs connect the mechanical causes of face drift (rotation, toe hang, CG position, face angle errors) to the solutions that eliminate them through engineering rather than hand manipulation.

The Confidence-Performance Doom Loop: These pairs connect the psychological and neurological consequences of inconsistent face control (yips, pressure anxiety, practice-to-course gap) to the confidence restoration that face-stable engineering delivers.

The Putting Identity Crisis: These pairs connect the experience of good ball-strikers trapped by bad putting to the identity resolution that comes from discovering the problem was equipment physics, not personal skill.
The Putter Carousel: These pairs connect the exhausting cycle of buying and discarding putters to the experience of finding a fundamentally different design that ends the search permanently.

The Zero-Torque Awakening and Its Limitations: These pairs connect the specific shortcomings of existing zero-torque implementations to the market opportunity for a face-stable design that delivers the benefit without the tradeoffs.

The Data vs Feel Divide: These pairs connect quantifiable mechanical problems to solutions described in both data terms (degrees, strokes saved) and feel terms (soft, confident, pendulum), serving both buyer types.

The Premium Price Reckoning: These pairs connect wasted money on failed solutions (fittings, carousel purchases, technique fixes) to the value proposition of solving the root cause with a single properly engineered purchase.
